name: Acorn Squash Corn Bread Dressing
recipeIngredient: 12 slices Stale white bread, ends trimmed
2 Red or green delicious Apples, peeled and chopped
3 stalks Celery, strings removed, cut into 1/2 inch dices
2 and three quarters cups Low sodium chicken broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Slice acorn squash in half, dot with 1 tablespoon butter, and bake for 40 minutes. Cool, remove skin, chop into 1/2 inch cubes.
3. In a mixing bowl, coarsely crumble stale bread and mix with crumbled corn bread. Mix in apples and raisins.
4. Melt remaining butter in a heavy skillet; add onions and celery. Saute until tender. Add to crumbs.
5. Mix in thyme, sage, salt, pepper, and squash.
6. Add beaten eggs and chicken broth. Mix well.
7. Spray a casserole dish with cooking spray. Spread mixture evenly in dish.
8. Bake for 45 minutes. Makes 12 servings.
